item.title: Transcriptional and ubiquitinative suppression of macrophage CST3 disrupts colonic homeostasis through defective efferocytosis.
item.description: Cell death and differentiation - To elucidate the role and regulatory mechanisms of macrophage-derived cystatin C (CST3) in Crohn's disease (CD), focusing on colonic inflammation, macrophage-epithelial interactions, and barrier dy...

item.title: The Impact of Metabolic Bariatric Surgery on Inflammatory Bowel Disease Risk and Outcomes in Adults With Obesity: a Propensity-matched, Nationwide, Analysis.
item.description: Obesity surgery - The risk of de novo inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) after metabolic bariatric surgery (MBS) has been described, but the timing and severity of de novo IBD is unclear. Using MarketScan Databases, p...

item.title: Pattern-based histologic approach in colitis without chronic architectural damage: GIPAD recommendations.
item.description: Digestive and liver disease : official journal of the Italian Society of Gastroenterology and the Italian Association for the Study of the Liver - Review - In patients presenting with intestinal symptoms who undergo colonoscopy with mucosal sampling, the pathologist plays a central role in identifying the underlying etiology in order to guide appropri...

item.title: EUS-guided drainage for pelvic abscesses: a Chinese single-center two-year follow-up study highlighting clinical outcomes.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Endoscopic ultrasound (EUS)- guided drainage has emerged as a novel technique for managing pelvic abscesses. This single-center retrospective case series aims to assess the safety and efficacy of E...

item.title: Too late to operate? Early surgical assessment in the era of advanced medical therapy for Crohn's disease.
item.description: Colorectal disease : the official journal of the Association of Coloproctology of Great Britain and Ireland - The expanding availability of advanced medical therapies has profoundly reshaped the management of Crohn's disease. However, an unintended consequence may be the progressive deferral of surgical in...

item.title: Recommendations for the Evaluation and Management of Inflammatory Bowel Disease with Irritable Bowel Syndrome-Like Symptoms: A Joint Rome Foundation and International Organization for the Study of IBD (IOIBD) Consensus.
item.description: Gastroenterology - A substantial proportion of persons with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) in remission continue to experience abdominal pain, altered bowel habits, and bloating that resemble irritable bowel syndro...

item.title: Outcomes Following Repair of Enterocutaneous Fistulae at a Specialized Center.
item.description: Journal of gastrointestinal surgery : official journal of the Society for Surgery of the Alimentary Tract - Enterocutaneous fistula (ECF) is a serious condition that can be challenging to treat with high recurrence and mortality rate. This study reports the characteristics and outcomes of a large series ...

item.title: Staged Surgical Management of a Colosplenic Fistula Caused by Locally Advanced Splenic-Flexure Adenocarcinoma.
item.description: Clinical case reports - Colosplenic fistula is a rare complication most commonly associated with colonic lymphoma, adenocarcinoma, and Crohn's disease. In unstable and septic patients, a staged strategy consisting of init...

item.title: Dynamic profiling in inflammatory Bowel disease: A manifesto for personalized care.
item.description: Autoimmunity reviews - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), encompassing Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, is a heterogeneous chronic condition whose clinical management has been advanced by the availability of molecu...

item.title: Dysregulated IL-7/IL-7R-CD132 Axis and Intestinal Microsporidiosis in Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Pathogens (Basel, Switzerland) - Crohn's disease (CD) is frequently accompanied by T-cell lymphopenia and impaired mucosal immunity, conditions that may predispose to intestinal microsporidiosis by Encephalitozoon cuniculi. This p...

item.title: A model for Crohn's disease post-ileal resection fibrosis development using human ileal enteroids and myofibroblasts.
item.description: Frontiers in physiology - Fibrosis and inflammation frequently emerge soon after ileal resection for strictures in Crohn's disease (CD). This study examined the profibrotic and proinflammatory effects of conditioned media f...
